entries.scope_summary: Make `ana proof <slug>` *pop*. The proof card is the product's signature artifact — the receipt that proves an AI-built feature was scoped, planned, built, and independently verified, with per-agent turns/tokens/cost computed deterministically and committed to git. Nobody else can produce this data. But the **presentation is dated**: bold labels over dashed-underline rules, flat one-line-per-item lists, a hard `... and N more` truncation, every assertion listed (46 green lines for a large feature), and a Provenance cost that looks like bad math because the cache tokens that justify it are captured but never shown.

findings.summary: Ad-hoc bold sub-header `chalk.bold('  Phase breakdown')` still present inside formatHumanReadable. AC2 says no inline section-header construction should remain; this sub-header (inside the Timing block, multi-phase path) was not converted to a primitive. Carried from the FAIL round — never a blocker (AC2 was PARTIAL), and a defensible call since it is a sub-header inside a section, not a top-level section header.

Golden test pins the timezone by mutating process.env['TZ']='UTC' in beforeAll and restoring in afterAll. Correct and robust for this file (verified green under UTC/Tokyo/New_York, and full suite green under TZ=UTC). But process.env.TZ is process-global: if vitest ever runs this file in a worker shared with another time-dependent test file, that file could transiently observe UTC during this file's run. No leak observed in practice; the restore is clean.

Counts-unavailable session (derived absent) renders as a standalone `<label>  counts unavailable` line above the grid (proof.ts:452-455), not as an in-grid row spanning the numeric columns as the spec mockup depicts. This is arguably cleaner (keeps numeric columns from widening) and the substance — the session is shown loudly and contributes nothing to totals — is fully met. No contract assertion governs the exact rendering.

Stale `@ana A020` tag points at the wrong assertion. In this contract A020 is the findings-overflow no-bare-and-N-more rule, but the tag sits on the single-phase phase-breakdown test in an unmodified file. Pre-existing mis-tag; harmless here (A020 is correctly covered by the golden test at line 251) but would mislead tag-driven verification. Carried from the FAIL round; the fix cycle did not touch this file.

Contract file_changes lists proofSummary.test.ts and commit-hygiene.test.ts as 'modify', but their substring toContain assertions survive the new format with minimal/no change. Harmless over-prediction; a planner could mark such resilient files as optional. Carried from the FAIL round.
findings.closed_reason: upstream

previous_failures.summary: Provenance-absent golden snapshot failed under UTC (TZ-dependent timestamp)
≥6-session golden snapshot failed under UTC
FAIL/DEVIATED golden failed under UTC (`22:40` vs baked `16:40`)

build_concerns.summary: AC2: one chalk.bold('Phase breakdown') Timing sub-header remains instead of a sectionRule (it is a sub-section of Timing, not a top-level card section, and proofSummary.test.ts asserts the token so it must change in lockstep). Explicitly out of scope for the fix cycle by agreement.

Pre-existing @ana tag-ID collision: proof.test.ts and proof.ts carry @ana A0xx tags from the ORIGINAL proof contract, while this feature's contract reuses A001-A031 for different assertions. A verifier's tag scan could mis-map the old tags. This feature's genuine coverage lives in render.test.ts (A001-A011) and proof-card-golden.test.ts + the A028 test (A012-A031).

Stale @ana A020 tag in proofSummary.test.ts:2409 points at the phase-breakdown test, but A020 in this contract is the findings-overflow rule. Pre-existing mis-tag in a file this build does not modify; A020 is correctly covered by the golden test. Flagged for a future tag sweep.

Finding / Build-Concern summaries are free text and are not width-bounded in the card; a pathological long summary could exceed 80 columns. The card's structural layout (header box, provenance grid, rules, footer) stays <=80 across all seven golden fixtures. The statGrid/keyValueRows truncation primitives are the eventual fix when health adopts them (cli-polish-C3).
Pre-existing lint warning at git-operations.ts:198 (unused eslint-disable directive). Not a file this build touches; flagged pre-existing by the verifier in cycle 1. Not introduced by this build.

entries.scope_summary: After `proof-card-redesign` ships, `proof.ts` will contain **three** human renderers: the redesigned proof card (`formatHumanReadable`), the health dashboard (`formatHealthDisplay`), and the no-slug summary table (`formatListTable`). Leaving them dated means `ana proof health` and `ana proof` look like a different, older product than `ana proof <slug>` one screen away. *Finished means consistent.*

entries.scope_summary: Replace Anatomia's hand-rolled transcript forensics with the published `anatrace-core` package, then build a behavioral-attestation layer on top of it. In the user's words: "properly use and test anatrace within anatomia... use the real anatrace package, test it, and even expand what we currently do by leveraging anatrace."

findings.summary: Golden snapshot fixture INPUTS were changed (cache_read 80k→1M, 900k→1M; model gpt-5-codex→gpt-5) to keep the card within 80 columns once the wider real table-version label is shown — so the golden test no longer proves cost-invariance for unchanged inputs.

captureProvenanceAtSave no longer calls deriveTranscript — it re-reads bytes and calls deriveCountsFromBytes directly so the transcript_hash attests the same bytes (read-once). deriveTranscript is now reachable only from tests. Intentional, but the read-bytes+basename+derive sequence is duplicated across the two functions.

AC13 totality (a core call throwing mid-capture must not break the save) has no explicit test that forces parseSession/deriveCounts to throw. Covered structurally by the outer try/catch in captureProvenanceAtSave and by the unreadable-transcript omit test, but not directly exercised.

Stray indentation in the prov() shape helper: derive_version sits at 6 spaces while sibling keys are at 8. Lint passes (eslint indent not enforced inside this object literal) but it is inconsistent with the file.

parseTestCounts best-effort regex (session-capture-C12) is resolved: the helper is deleted and test/failure counting is delegated to anatrace-core's command-tool-gated derive. Codex files_touched=0 build concern is also resolved (A008 now derives from a real patch_apply_end body).
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: harness_version is still recorded empty — the session-capture build concern is NOT addressed by this phase (the spec explicitly defers filling it from the transcript version key to Phase 2). Noted so it is not assumed closed.

resolveTranscriptPath remains exported with zero external importers (cross-machine-provenance-C2 still present). Not introduced by this build and not in scope; the refactor correctly added no NEW zero-importer exports.
A023 scrub test cannot isolate the scrub mechanism — the record shape already excludes transcript bytes, so the test passes even if scrubDeep were removed
Malformed-but-readable transcript branch (parseSession returns null) is never exercised; the A022 totality test uses the unreadable-file path instead, leaving compliance.ts:193 uncovered
buildRootLaneContext boundary param is inert (void boundary) — accepted and threaded from the call site but has zero effect on output today; spec-sanctioned future seam

Spec referenced canonicalSort for the mandate hash, but the build hashes source-blob bytes (byte-identity) instead — aligns better with AC17; canonicalSort import is unused. Worth acknowledging on next seal
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: readCoreVersion returns '' on failure; A020 ('exists') would still pass with an empty string, so a record could carry an empty engine version while satisfying the assertion

tests/commands/init/template-propagation.test.ts is flaky under full-suite parallel load (5000ms timeout; passes in isolation at ~9s). Pre-existing, unrelated to Phase 2 — raise its timeout or split it

build_concerns.summary: @ana tag namespace is not contract-scoped: all-scaffolds.test.ts and proofSummary.test.ts reuse A001–A012 for an unrelated contract, colliding with this contract's Phase-1 ids.
A002 (forensics has no hand-rolled deriveClaude) has no dedicated unit test — satisfied by deletion plus the contract's mechanical not_contains source check, not an @ana-tagged test.

Two timing-sensitive tests (parsing-performance cache-speedup ≥0.80; init template-propagation 5s timeout) flake under concurrent machine load; both pass in isolation and are untouched by this work. Not regressions.

AC6 fix surfaced a pre-existing latent proof-card layout overflow: the TOTAL footer trailing '(table 2026-06-08)' reaches 81–83 cols for unpriced multi-session runs. Rendering left untouched per spec; two golden stress fixtures trimmed so the realistic card fits 80 cols (user-approved).

deriveCountsFromBytes casts the string harness to core's Harness union (as Harness). A harness value other than 'claude'/'codex' would still be passed to parseSession, which falls back to shape detection — benign, but the cast hides the narrowing.
captureComplianceAtSave outer try-catch is not separately unit-triggered; only early-return no-record paths are tested (no external input reliably forces a mid-pipeline core throw)

parseSession never returns null for in-band garbage — the 'malformed transcript → no record' path is exercised via an unreadable (dangling) transcript, not corrupt bytes; corrupt-but-readable transcripts still produce a record
artifact.ts provenancePaths list now holds both compliance and provenance file paths; behavior is correct but the variable name reads narrower than its contents
Pre-existing flaky test (not introduced by this build): tests/commands/init/template-propagation.test.ts times out at 5s under full-suite load, passes 21/21 in isolation

anatomiaAdapter does not emit subject.delegates:'include' claims from current Anatomia agent-defs; the A014 delegate-inclusive soundness test augments a real adapter claim's subject (documented deviation). If agent-defs gain delegate-inclusive phrasing the test should switch to native adapter output

entries.feature: Bump anatrace-core 0.2.0 → 0.4.0 (pin, fail-closed emit, reason lock, real-engine CI)

assertions.says: The behavioral engine is installed at version 0.4.0

A reason the engine does not produce is flagged as unknown

When the engine version cannot be resolved, no attestation is written

The recorded engine version matches the engine that actually judged the session
An obfuscated forbidden command is caught as a violation under the real engine

entries.scope_summary: Anatomia's behavioral-attestation layer is fully wired but has emitted **zero** records, because the engine it consumes (`anatrace-core`) is pinned to a version that is both **uninstalled** and **known-false-PASS** (0.2.0). The product's headline differentiator is therefore undemonstrated, and emitting on 0.2.0/0.3.0 would attest *behind a forgeable engine*.

findings.summary: Contract file_changes under-scoped the bump ripple — 0.4.0 ships a new PRICE_TABLE_VERSION (2026-06-08 → 2026-06-14) that forced edits to 5 test files the contract never listed (_capture, pricing, forensics, forensics-derive, proof-card-golden + snapshot).
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: Spec A055 guidance was factually wrong about where the no-force-push obligation lives — it said use the build mandate (ana-build.md), but the forbidden-command predicate is the engine's built-in VERIFY_FORBIDDEN_COMMANDS for the VERIFY role. Builder correctly adapted to the verify role; following the spec literally would have produced a fixture that never flips to violated.
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: projectVerdicts default param `coreVersion: string = readCoreVersion()` re-invokes the resolver. The sole production caller passes coreVersion explicitly so it never fires today, but a future caller relying on the default would bypass the fail-closed gate and interpolate an empty `anatrace-core@` into the drift warning.

Real-engine happy-path tests (A052/A053/A054) judge a trivial 'doing work' transcript. A053 guards with verdicts.length > 0 before asserting zero out-of-set reasons, so it cannot pass vacuously — a good defensive assertion worth preserving if the fixture is ever simplified further.

Observable (non-gating): no compliance record with anatrace_core_version == 0.4.0 is on disk yet for this cycle — it emits at `ana artifact save`, not at test time. Expected to land when the verify report is saved. Absence is a ~5-min follow-on per spec, never a held PR.

C12 confirmed FIXED by this build — the fail-closed gate computes the engine version once and abstains (returns null, writes no record) on empty/unresolvable, so no anatrace_core_version: '' record can ever land. Build concern (abstain path had no reliable external trigger) is now ADDRESSED via the deps.readCoreVersion seam.
findings.closed_reason: upstream

build_concerns.summary: anatrace-core 0.4.0 also moved PRICE_TABLE_VERSION (2026-06-08 → 2026-06-14); cost-metric layer re-baselined as a cosmetic stamp here. Prices themselves are unchanged, but the metric layer should be consciously revalidated.

Spec AC4(iii) names ana-build.md as the no-force-push mandate source, but the obligation is the engine's built-in VERIFY_FORBIDDEN_COMMANDS for the VERIFY role (anatrace-core index.mjs:5265,5463). The build mandate has no command predicate; the A055 fixture uses the verify mandate. Spec/plan understanding should be corrected for future cycles.

Spec scoped only compliance.test.ts among test files and never ran the full suite against 0.4.0 (baseline was estimated by counting it() blocks). The bump in fact broke 11 tests across 5 files (price-table stamp + the _capture pin), all fixed here. Future bump specs should run the suite before scoping.

Non-gating observable (a compliance record with anatrace_core_version == 0.4.0) had not yet landed on disk at build time — it emits at ana artifact save, not ana test. Verify after the build-report save; absence is a ~5-min follow-on ana run per the spec, never a held PR.

entries.scope_summary: Make the contract's fidelity to scope intent **mechanically trustless**. Today the contract is positioned as the authoritative specification, but it is only a *fallible proxy* for what the scope actually asked for. Whether built code satisfies intent rests on the planner's diligence translating intent→assertions and the verifier's disposition to *notice* a thin translation — with **no mechanical check that every acceptance criterion is even covered, and no gating outlet when a gap is found.**

findings.summary: Flaky test in the broader suite — one test failed in the sealed verify run (3766p/1f/2s) but never reproduced across 7 full-suite runs + 5 regression-focus runs. 'Push failed after retry' noise points to a git-operation/retry test, not Phase 1 code.

scope-ac-corpus.test.ts asserts toBe(0) against the live, growing completed-scope corpus (205 today). A future completed scope using '## Acceptance Criteria' with non-AC-id criteria would flip emptyExtractionCount/falseAmbiguousCount and break this test in an unrelated future PR. Intentional safety gate, but couples future greens to historical scope formatting.

Bold-form regex /\*\*\s*(AC\d+)/ matches **ACn** anywhere on a line, so a prose mention ('see **AC3** above') extracts AC3 as a criterion id. Harmless across the 205-scope corpus and the dogfood (exactly AC1-14, no spurious ids), but a future version-1.1 contract could gain a spurious prose-derived id that becomes a false 'uncovered' block. Latent, low-likelihood.

Defensive try/catch around joinCoverage in evaluateCoverageGate is effectively unreachable — joinCoverage is total (no throw path), so the 'could not evaluate' diagnostic branch cannot trigger in practice and no test exercises it. Spec explicitly requested defensive depth, so it is intentional, but it is untested dead-ish code.
Stale/cross-contract @ana tags in long-lived test files mis-map this contract's assertion IDs
proofSummary.ts continues to grow past the comfort threshold (Phase 2 adds coverage threading)
parseACResults PARTIAL regex false-match risk only partially mitigated; section-scoping helps but in-section bullets containing PARTIAL in prose could still match
plan.ts missing-slug guard prints 'Error:' to stderr but exits 0 — an error condition reports success exit code
Spec file_changes under-specified the test plan — A024/A027 (cardOutput) and the PR PARTIAL surface required proof.test.ts and pr.test.ts edits the spec did not list; builder added them correctly
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: plan.ts re-parses coverage_waivers for reason text because joinCoverage does not return the reason; minor duplication of waiver iteration

build_concerns.summary: The gate's blocking path through artifact.ts (print errors + process.exit(1)) is covered only at the unit level (evaluateCoverageGate). No integration test drives saveArtifact/saveAllArtifacts to an actual block, because the gate is inert by design — no version 1.1 contract exists to trigger it until Phase 2. The artifact.ts layer is a thin print+exit mirror of applyTestEvidenceGate.

AC6 (scope: 'a contract with no assertions never triggers the gate, active:false') and AC13 ('a version 1.1 contract with zero ac links over a scope with ACs reports active:true, block:true') are in tension for the zero-assertion + version-1.1 case. Resolved per the contract's explicit pins (A020/A021): activation = version>=1.1 AND scope-has-ACs AND not-ambiguous, independent of assertion count. AC6's no-assertions clause holds in the legacy 1.0 regime and via the no-AC-scope path (A017).

extractScopeACs recovers ids only from the four structural forms (bullet, heading, bold, bare label). An AC referenced only mid-prose without bold (e.g. 'see AC3') is intentionally not recovered, for precision. Acceptable while the gate is dormant; worth awareness when Phase 2 activates real contracts.
Syncing root dogfood agents to templates changes the agents this repo itself uses on next re-init (two-gate verifier, 1.1-emitting planner).
`ana plan coverage` is advisory, not an automatic pipeline step; real enforcement is the Phase 1 gate at `ana artifact save`. Don't mistake the preview for a gate.
plan.ts reads contract.coverage_waivers directly for waiver reason display (joinCoverage returns status, not reason) — a second site touching the waiver shape.
Spec File Changes under-specified the .codex template + dogfood edits; the diff touches more files than the table lists (followed the spec's Both-Claude-and-Codex constraint + existing sync tests).

entries.feature: Verifier Verdict Honesty (light) — the PASS/FAIL verdict stops grading itself

assertions.says: The verifier's instructions no longer tell it to read the build report
The Codex verifier's instructions also no longer tell it to read the build report
The verifier is still told it must never read the build report
The verifier can still check untested work by inspecting the source
The shipped and in-repo copies of the verifier definition stay identical
A clean PASS verdict stays PASS
A PASS that contradicts the verifier's own unsatisfied row is overridden to FAIL
The override explains which unsatisfied row contradicted the PASS
A FAIL verdict stays FAIL
A report with no verdict line is reported as unknown, not assumed passing
Every place that reads the verdict gets the overridden result, not the raw headline
Reading the verdict from a saved file also returns the overridden result
The PR step also sees the overridden result
The verdict is parsed in exactly one place, not copied across the codebase
When a contradicted PASS blocks completion, the reason is shown to the developer
A verify report missing its verdict line is still rejected at save time
The verdict code states the honest boundary: not one-word-forgeable, still self-authored
The code never claims the verdict makes the agent unable to lie
If the verifier deterministically read the build report, the proof is forced to FAIL
A satisfied verify-independence claim does not trigger the veto
A non-deterministic (LLM-judged or unmarked) verdict never triggers the veto
The veto fires on exactly one claim — other claims and other roles never gate
The determinism signal is recorded on the verdict so the veto can read it
Verdicts recorded before this feature (no determinism signal) never retroactively gate
With no captured transcript the veto is skipped openly, never silently
A verify session that read the build report cannot complete with a PASS
The proof record states whether the byte-level veto was applied
The verifier docs no longer claim behavioral verdicts can never affect PASS/FAIL
The proof states the veto is forward-only and earlier verdicts were self-reported

entries.scope_summary:
Make Anatomia's PASS/FAIL verdict mean what the product says it means. Today the headline
verdict is regex-scraped from a prose `**Result:**` line the verify *model* typed — so the
brand claim ("trust the bytes, not the model") is, at the verdict itself, still
trust-the-model. The whole product is positioned on "Verify never grades its own homework";
the verdict is the one place that promise is currently only *hoped*.

findings.summary: Circular import between verdict.ts and proofSummary.ts — verdict.ts imports parseComplianceTable, proofSummary.ts imports deriveVerdict
@ana tags A001-A005 are duplicated within agent-proof-context.test.ts (prior merged contract + this contract) — tag-by-id resolution is ambiguous in this file

deriveVerdict coerces only on an exactly-spelled 'UNSATISFIED' status; a typo'd/garbled status cell falls back to 'UNKNOWN' (proofSummary.ts:205) and will NOT gate a PASS — consistent with the self-authored honesty boundary but fragile

deriveVerdict never coerces a PASS on DEVIATED or UNCOVERED rows — only UNSATISFIED. By design (coverage gating is the separate verifier-intent-coverage feature), but the contradiction signal is intentionally narrow
Pre-existing lint warning (unused eslint-disable directive) in git-operations.ts:198 — not in this build's file_changes, not a regression
Three type docstrings still assert verdicts are 'EVIDENCE, NEVER A GATE' unconditionally, now imprecise for the one allowlisted veto claim

User-facing incomplete-coverage warning says 'behavioral verdicts are evidence, never a gate' unqualified, while its sibling display copy in proof.ts:670 was correctly qualified with the veto exception

Spec gotcha enumerated only compliance.ts + proof.ts for the evidence->gate copy flip; types/proof.ts (3 docstrings) and the work-proof.ts stderr warning also carried the invariant but were not listed, so the contradiction the spec warned about leaked into unlisted files
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: A verify session that reads build_report.md via Bash/Grep substring (not the Read tool) is never exercised in Anatomia's own suite — relied on as an anatrace-core engine guarantee (read-paths binds only to Read file_path); a future engine bump could regress it undetected here

build_concerns.summary: Name collision: utils/verdict.ts exports deriveVerdict (content -> VerdictResult) while utils/capture-runner.ts already exports a different deriveVerdict (counts, exitCode -> CaptureVerdict). No file imports both, so no compile conflict, but the shared name is confusing. The spec/contract hardcode the name 'deriveVerdict' for the verify-report function, so renaming was not an option.

Function-only circular import introduced: proofSummary.ts imports deriveVerdict from verdict.ts, and verdict.ts imports parseComplianceTable from proofSummary.ts. ESM resolves this because neither symbol is touched at module-load (only inside function bodies). artifact-validators.ts -> verdict.ts -> proofSummary.ts -> artifact-validators.ts (joinCoverage) is the same shape and already existed for joinCoverage. Build, typecheck, and full suite all green, but the cycle is a latent fragility if any of these modules later needs a top-level use of an imported symbol.

readLocalVerifyResult (artifact.ts) and extractVerifyResult (pr.ts) were changed from private to exported solely so contract assertions A014/A015 could have direct tagged unit tests. Both were previously untested. This widens the module surface slightly; the project's risk profile already notes proof.ts/proof-summary over-exporting tendencies.

Pre-existing lint warning in src/utils/git-operations.ts:198 (unused eslint-disable directive for no-control-regex) surfaces on every pre-commit run. Not introduced by this build and only a warning, but it rides along in the lint output.

AC5's proof-entry half (verdict_contradictions rendering on a completed proof entry) is covered by type + writeProofChain wiring and source inspection, not by an end-to-end fixture that completes work with a coerced-PASS report and asserts the field lands in proof_chain.json. The guard-message half IS unit-tested (A017). Verifier should assess whether source-level evidence suffices for the 'on the proof entry' clause.

A016, A019, A020 are verified by source-content tests that read verdict.ts / proofSummary.ts and assert on substrings. These are inherently comment/format-fragile (the project's own risk profile flags a similar purity test as fragile). They faithfully cover the not_contains/contains intent but will break on benign rewording.
Pre-existing display test assertion updated to AC6 copy (verdicts no longer 'never a gate' unconditionally); spec-driven expected-value change, not a weakened matcher.

A written proof_chain entry's verdict_veto.applied is always false — guardVerdictVeto exits before the entry is written (mirrors guardFailResult). The APPLIED render path is reachable only via test-constructed entries, not writeProofChain.

Stale @ana tags from prior contracts (anatrace-core-integration / anatrace-pin) remain in compliance.test.ts and proof-compliance-display.test.ts; same-numbered tags do NOT map to this contract's assertions. Component-3 tests use current IDs.

entries.feature: Guard the anatrace-core load and emit the first real attestation records

assertions.says: When the attestation engine can't be loaded, saving abstains instead of crashing
A missing attestation engine never throws out of the save path
A missing attestation engine surfaces a loud, visible warning instead of failing silently
Routine skips like no role or no session stay quiet — the missing-engine warning is not a false alarm
An empty engine version makes attestation abstain rather than stamp a bogus record
When the engine version is empty, no attestation file is written to disk
The recorded engine version matches the engine package actually installed
A real attestation always stamps a non-empty engine version
The attestation engine is pinned and installed at exactly version 0.4.0, no caret or tilde
A real attestation under the live engine contains at least one behavioral verdict
A real attestation reports non-zero coverage of the behavioral mandate
Every verdict reason the live 0.4.0 engine emits is a recognized reason

entries.scope_summary: Anatomia's headline differentiator — behavioral attestation — has never produced a record. Two defects, one work item:

findings.summary: loadCore deviates from spec's prescribed bare-require idiom — resolves package.json, reads exports['.'].import, and require()s the ESM entry by absolute path. Necessary (anatrace-core is import-only ESM; bare require throws ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED) and well-commented; proven working by the emitted build record.

Node version portability: loadCore uses require() on an ESM .mjs entry. Unflagged require(ESM) landed in Node 22.12.0; README states 'Node 22+'. On Node 22.0-22.11 an installed engine would throw ERR_REQUIRE_ESM, get caught, and falsely surface the loud 'anatrace-core not resolvable' line. Works on current toolchain (Node 25 here).

Spec's documented edge 'core present but package.json unreadable -> loadCore succeeds, version guard abstains silently' is no longer true. loadCore now reads package.json to find the ESM entry, so an unreadable package.json yields a LOUD abstain, not a silent version abstain. The version guard's production reachability narrows to a present-but-missing/non-string version field (plus the test injection seam). Arguably more correct, but deviates from documented semantics.

Tag drift: this contract's A009 (package.json anatrace-core dependency == '0.4.0') has no matching @ana A009 tag. The pin test that actually enforces it (tests/commands/_capture.test.ts:220) carries stale IDs '@ana A001, A045, A046' from a prior cycle's contract. A009 verified by source inspection (pin literal '0.4.0', store resolves anatrace-core@0.4.0), but the tag linkage is broken.

Quiet-direction test (A004) covers only the no-role benign path; spec named 'no role OR no session'. loadCore: () => null is injected but never invoked because the role guard short-circuits first — so the test cannot distinguish a correctly-quiet benign path from a broken loud guard. It correctly pins the ordering intent, but is single-path.

captureComplianceAtSave's outer try/catch (compliance.ts:237-359) swallows any mid-pipeline core throw (parseSession/extract/runCompliance/scrubDeep) into a silent null abstain. The reorder preserves this; the catch path remains not separately unit-triggered. Pre-existing, not introduced here.

build_concerns.summary: Spec/Build-Brief's literal loadCore idiom createRequire('anatrace-core') cannot work — anatrace-core is import-only ESM (no require condition in exports), so the bare specifier throws ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED. Working loader resolves the package.json export then require()s the ESM entry by absolute path (Node 25 require(esm), still synchronous).
Pre-existing lint warning (Unused eslint-disable directive 'no-control-regex') in git-operations.ts:198 — not introduced by this build, out of scope, left untouched.

Dogfood record emit depends on the session env (ANA_ROLE/run-id/session-id) AND a freshly rebuilt main-tree dist on installed core. A stale dist or absent core silently re-emits zero — the exact fragility Move 1 makes loud. Verify confirms the record on disk.

Move 2 mutated the main tree's node_modules (pnpm install of anatrace-core@0.4.0) and rebuilt packages/cli/dist. Ratified by spec (Build owns sequencing), flagged before build. The dist reflects main-tree source (old un-guarded import) + installed core until merge.

assertions.says: The verifier chip describes Verify as fault-finding, not as a machine that computes the verdict
The 'Mechanical, not vibes' section title is left unchanged
The 'No LLM grades its own code' line is left unchanged
The manifesto's 'you read the chain' line is left unchanged

entries.scope_summary:
Three honesty touch-ups on Anatomia's public surface — two corrections and one
documentation gap. A product whose entire pitch is *"verified over trusted"*
cannot ship a surface that overclaims. Each fix removes a statement the code
doesn't honor; none touch legitimate marketing.

findings.summary: Fix 1 replaces a false CI claim but introduces another — 'coverage gates' is not honored by CI. test.yml:63 runs `pnpm vitest run` with no --coverage flag, and vitest.config.ts defines thresholds but never sets coverage.enabled:true, so coverage thresholds are dormant in CI. The phrase was prescribed verbatim by scope+spec (AC1), so the fix belongs upstream, not in Build.
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: Contract A004 value 'You don' is a weak substring (matches 'You donate...' as readily as 'You don't'). The guard test wisely over-delivers — it also asserts 'have to trust the model. You read the chain.' — but the contract's pinned value itself is low-bar. Future contracts should pin a more distinctive substring.
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: Pre-existing lint warnings in website (formatAge unused in components/hero or lib/proof-feed; 'latest' unused). 0 errors, gate passes. NOT introduced by this build — the four changed files contain neither symbol. Recorded so the next engineer doesn't attribute them to this change.

Proof context shows two still-present concerns on website/lib/copy.ts from prior cycles: manifesto outbound link points to /#pipeline (no longer exists), and proofFeed copy references clickable rows that are no longer clickable. This build does not touch either — still present, out of scope here.

Fix 3 docs section summarizes the coverage-gate activation as 'on a scope whose criteria it can parse', folding in the underlying '>=1 recovered AC' condition rather than enumerating it. This is per explicit spec instruction (do not overstate the gate). Accurate and deliberately terse — noted, not a defect.

build_concerns.summary: Website vitest baseline showed 1 pre-existing failure (docs-platform-content.test.ts → ENOENT public/search-index.json) when run in a fresh worktree before the Next build generated the asset. Not introduced by this build; resolves once `pnpm --filter anatomia-website check` (or any build) regenerates the search index. Final sealed `ana test` run shows 0 failures.
Pre-existing lint warnings (formatAge, latest unused) remain in the website lint output (0 errors, 2 warnings). They are in files outside this spec's four targets and were not touched.

entries.feature: Proof-Context Intelligence — why this file exists and what moves with it

assertions.says: Proof context tells you which verified work items shaped a file
Each shaping work item carries its slug, kind, date, and intent
The proof context output shows a 'Shaped by' section
When more than three work items shaped a file, a drill-down hint points to the cheap detail view
The --why view shows the work item's intent
The --why view hides provenance noise like cost and token accounting
The --why view hides timing breakdowns
A long work-item intent is truncated, not dumped whole, in Shaped by
Proof context lists other files that change together with this one
The output shows an 'Also changes with' section
When more co-change partners exist than are shown, a 'top N' footer says so
With an import graph present, proof context shows who imports a file
The import blast-radius layer renders even with no proof history
A file's own test file is not listed as something that changes with it
When a test partner is suppressed, the output says so in one line
Each co-change partner is labelled by how it relates to the import graph
With no import graph, a partner is honestly 'unknown', never guessed
Querying with no import graph never crashes
A file touched fewer than three times never appears as a co-change couple
A co-change couple needs at least two shared work items to appear
One giant refactor touching many files cannot manufacture co-change pairs by itself
With no proof history, the 'Shaped by' section is absent, not faked
With no history and no graph, 'Also changes with' is absent entirely
Existing proof-context fields still work after the new ones are added
The existing findings list is unchanged by the new sections
A hot file with dozens of partners still shows only the top few
A hot file's output stays a first-screen, with an overflow footer
A fresh project install writes the import graph so blast-radius works day one
Reading a missing import graph returns nothing instead of crashing
The scoping agent is told to run proof context on the files a change touches
The reviewer no longer treats proof history as an optional aside
The reviewer is told to form findings independently of the shaped-by history
The planner passes co-change partners into the Build Brief so the builder won't forget them
The same guidance reaches Codex users, not just Claude users

entries.scope_summary: Turn `ana proof context {files}` from a "what's wrong here" lookup into the orientation surface an agent reads before touching code. It already returns `findings + build_concerns + touch_count + last_touched` (`proofSummary.ts:1147`). Add the two highest-value questions an agent has and can't answer anywhere else:

findings.summary: Contract file_changes omits the repo-root dogfood agent copies (.claude/agents/*, .codex/agents/*) even though existing tests enforce byte-for-byte parity with templates; build correctly synced them but the contract under-specified the change surface
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: A006 (--why omits Provenance) has no positive-control guard asserting the full card DOES contain 'Provenance'; only A007's Timing has that guard, so A006 could silently pass if the Provenance label were renamed. Live-verified this round, but the test alone is weaker than A007's
--why flag is silently ignored when `ana proof` is invoked in list mode (no slug, no --last); harmless but the flag accepts input it does nothing with

proofSummary.ts (+32) and proof.ts (+113) continue growth past the comfort threshold flagged by prior findings (decompose-proof-summary-C1, audit-matrix-orientation-C7); additive and well-contained here but the trajectory persists

Pre-existing flaky test: template-propagation 'Claude-only never touches .codex tree' times out at ~5025ms under full-suite concurrency (passes 100% in isolation). Reproduced on main (failed 1/4 runs at 5025ms with no Phase 2 changes), so NOT a regression. Phase 2 adds import-graph build to every `ana init`, which may marginally raise the flake rate. Root cause: a single test spawns the real CLI `init` twice under the default 5000ms timeout. Fix: raise the timeout on these CLI-spawning init tests.

Spec-mandated `ana scan` byte-parity regression test is missing — no test asserts scanProject writes no code-graph.json when persistGraphTo is unset. The read-only contract is verified by source inspection only (guarded by `if (options.persistGraphTo)`).

New init integration test uses `git init` without `-b main`, violating the documented testing standard (CI runners vary init.defaultBranch — has caused CI failures 3 times). Mirrors the pre-existing flawed pattern in template-propagation.test.ts.

Stale harvested JSDoc: CodeGraph.filesAnalyzed is a `number` count but its JSDoc reads 'Files whose imports were considered (the parse universe), sorted' — describes an array. Doc drifted from the field during the verbatim harvest.

Harvested-but-unused surface in Phase 2: CodeGraph.barrelFiles, generatedFiles, inDegree and the readCodeGraph reader have zero src consumers this phase. By design — spec frames Phase 2 as an inert artifact consumed in Phase 3, and instructed harvesting the full type verbatim. Sanctioned, not YAGNI; flagged so Phase 3 closes the loop.

Init ordering asymmetry: code-graph.json is written (runAnalyzer, index.ts:131) before createDirectoryStructure (134), while symbol-index.json is written after (138). Safe today because createDirectoryStructure uses idempotent recursive mkdir, but a latent footgun if that ever clears the staging state dir.

AC3 path-form suppression bug from verify_report_3 is FIXED. isSameStemTestPartner now takes the pairing FileMatcher and routes the final comparison through match(normalizeForTestMatch(partner), normalizeForTestMatch(query)) instead of normalized exact-equality, so suppression is exactly as path-form-tolerant as pairing. Verified live: `node dist/index.js proof context src/utils/proofSummary.ts` (package-relative) and `... packages/cli/src/utils/proofSummary.ts` (repo-relative) BOTH render `top 3 of 39` + `(note: same-stem test partner suppressed)`; grep for proofSummary.test.ts in both outputs returns 0.

Path-form-mismatch test gap from verify_report_3 is FIXED. proof-history.test.ts:200 (@ana A014) exercises a package-relative query (src/commands/work.ts) against a repo-relative stored partner (packages/cli/tests/commands/work.test.ts) and asserts suppressedTestPartner === true, the test mirror absent, and the real partner present — the exact red-before/green-after case the prior fix brief specified. Suite went 4068 -> 4069 passing, 0 failed.

Contract A014/A015 text remains narrow: the assertions still describe the aligned-path case only. In practice the new @ana A014 unit test now exercises the path-form mismatch, closing the runtime gap, but the contract sentence itself does not mandate cross-path-form suppression. A future contract revision should encode 'suppression holds across path-form mismatch' so the guarantee is sealed, not just incidentally tested. Contract is sealed this cycle (INTACT) — do not edit now.
findings.closed_reason: upstream

findings.summary: Carried forward from verify_report_3 (unchanged — re-build touched only proof-history/index.ts). Import-layer dedup (isProofPartner) uses fileMatches, whose tier-3 basename rule returns true when a proof partner is stored as a bare basename (legacy data). A legacy bare-basename proof partner would suppress ALL same-basename files from the import layer regardless of directory. Low likelihood (requires legacy bare-basename modules_touched); silently drops real import edges if it occurs.

Carried forward from verify_report_3 (unchanged). Live run could not exercise the hidden/imports render path: this worktree has no .ana/state/code-graph.json, so every co-change partner renders under the `unknown` group (`Changed together:`). The graph-present render (hidden/imports grouping, imported_by/imports layers) is covered only by unit/integration tests that write a synthetic graph, not by an end-to-end run against the real repo. Reduced live confidence on that path only.

Carried forward from verify_report_3 (unchanged — the re-build added 0 lines to proofSummary.ts). proofSummary.ts remains oversized after Phase 3's ~138-line addition; proof context confirms decompose-proof-summary-C1 and audit-matrix-orientation-C7 are still active. The also_changes_with assembly/dedup glue could live in the pure engine module rather than the already-large util.

previous_failures.summary: SATISFIED but too narrow — tagged tests used aligned path forms, so AC3 intent failed for package-relative queries
SATISFIED for repo-relative only — no note emitted on the leaking package-relative query

build_concerns.summary: Pre-existing @ana tags (A001-A024) from a prior contract collide with this contract's IDs in proof.test.ts, proofSummary.test.ts, and agent-proof-context.test.ts; verifier should map tags from the new Phase-1 tests only.
Spec File Changes omitted the 6 repo-root dogfood agent copies (.claude/.codex), but the byte-for-byte dogfood-sync test required editing them in lockstep with the templates.
Truncated 140-char scope_summary can soft-wrap to a second physical line in narrow terminals — terminal wrapping, not a render bug; matches the spec mockup budget.
ParsedFile.file schema comment says 'relative to project root' but parseProjectFiles emits absolute paths; graph wiring relies on the absolute value
template-propagation.test.ts '.codex tree' case is flaky under full parallel load (passes in isolation and in the sealed run); pre-existing, in a touched module
Pre-existing unused eslint-disable directive warning surfaces in repo-wide lint; not introduced by this build

Fix cycle: isSameStemTestPartner now compares via fileMatches (same matcher as pairing) on normalized forms, so suppression inherits pairing's bare-basename/suffix tolerance — a query collapsing to a single bare segment (e.g. top-level src/index.ts) could match a same-basename test in another module. This is the tolerance the verify report asked for; the /-boundary protects all multi-segment paths.

template-propagation 'Claude-only never creates .codex' test runs two ana init subprocesses under the 5s default timeout — at the edge (5014/5430ms) and tipped over by added parallel subprocess load. Fixed with a 30s budget; sibling multi-init tests may share the pattern.
Pre-existing unused eslint-disable directive warning in git-operations.ts:198 — not introduced by this build, surfaces on every lint run.

Day-1 import layer and hidden/imports relation flags depend on code-graph.json, which is written at ana init/scan and not committed — absent in this worktree, so live `ana proof context` shows all partners as unknown with no import layer. Correct fail-soft behaviour; graph paths are exercised by fixture-graph tests only.

computeCoChange is recomputed per query inside getProofContext's map (touch counts walk all entries per query). Negligible for typical 1-3 file queries; O(files × entries) for a large multi-file query against a large chain.

ana-plan loads the testing-standards skill

Proof context tells you which verified work items shaped a file
Each shaping work item carries its slug, kind, date, and intent
The proof context output shows a 'Shaped by' section
When more than three work items shaped a file, a drill-down hint points to the cheap detail view
The --why view shows the work item's intent
The --why view hides provenance noise like cost and token accounting
The --why view hides timing breakdowns
A long work-item intent is truncated, not dumped whole, in Shaped by
Proof context lists other files that change together with this one
The output shows an 'Also changes with' section
When more co-change partners exist than are shown, a 'top N' footer says so
With an import graph present, proof context shows who imports a file
The import blast-radius layer renders even with no proof history
A file's own test file is not listed as something that changes with it
When a test partner is suppressed, the output says so in one line
Each co-change partner is labelled by how it relates to the import graph
With no import graph, a partner is honestly 'unknown', never guessed
Querying with no import graph never crashes
A file touched fewer than three times never appears as a co-change couple
A co-change couple needs at least two shared work items to appear
One giant refactor touching many files cannot manufacture co-change pairs by itself
With no proof history, the 'Shaped by' section is absent, not faked
With no history and no graph, 'Also changes with' is absent entirely
Existing proof-context fields still work after the new ones are added
The existing findings list is unchanged by the new sections
A hot file with dozens of partners still shows only the top few
A hot file's output stays a first-screen, with an overflow footer
A fresh project install writes the import graph so blast-radius works day one
Reading a missing import graph returns nothing instead of crashing
The scoping agent is told to run proof context on the files a change touches
The reviewer no longer treats proof history as an optional aside
The reviewer is told to form findings independently of the shaped-by history
The planner passes co-change partners into the Build Brief so the builder won't forget them
The same guidance reaches Codex users, not just Claude users
ana-build must load the git-workflow skill
ana-build never reads the build report

entries.feature: Empirical Proof Benchmark — the measuring instrument (ruler)

assertions.says: The benchmark suite is OFF by default and only turns on when explicitly enabled
The benchmark suite turns on only when the enable switch is set to exactly on
A stray or partial value never accidentally turns the benchmark suite on
The benchmark scorer never depends on the never-deploy branch it was ported from
The fixed localization task loads with its pinned commit and relevant-file set
Asking for a task that does not exist returns nothing instead of crashing
Scoring a real session transcript succeeds rather than abstaining
The scorer counts exactly how many distinct files the agent opened
The scorer counts how many of those files were off-target (the reliability signal)
Total tool calls come from the shipped provenance engine, not a re-implementation
The scorer records which model ran the session
Each scored run carries a dollar cost computed from the shipped price table
A run on a known model is marked as priced, so its cost is real, not a guess
An unknown model is marked unpriced rather than being shown as free
Reports how many file reads were wasteful repeats of files already opened
Reports the redundant-read ratio without dividing by zero on a no-read run
Reports the peak amount of context the agent held at once during the session
Reports peak context as a real percentage of the model's window, not a guess
On an edit task, reports the wall-clock time until the first correct edit
On an edit task, reports how many turns it took to reach the first correct edit
Breaks tokens into input, output, and cache columns for credible accounting
The cache token columns are present and separated from fresh input/output
On a read-only task, the time-to-edit metrics are blank rather than fabricated
An unreadable transcript is abstained on, never scored as a low-token win
An empty transcript abstains because there is no evidence to score
A transcript from an unsupported agent abstains rather than guessing
Averages a metric correctly across multiple runs of the same setup
Reports the spread (variance) of a metric across the runs of a cell
The 95% confidence interval is honest for small samples (wider than a naive interval)
A single run never crashes the stats — variance is flagged undefined, not invented
A single run is explicitly flagged so a reader knows variance is unavailable
Reports how often the Anatomia arm beat the bare arm across paired runs
Abstained runs are counted, never silently dropped from the totals
Given identical input, the aggregate output is byte-identical every time
The scorer accepts more than two benchmark arms without a breaking change
No credibility metric is built on the engine's flaky best-effort counts
The scorer never reads the best-effort files-touched count for any metric
The published measurement engine is consumed, never edited, for the benchmark

entries.scope_summary:
Harvest the benchmark harness/scorer from the never-deploy `feature/devday-scan`
branch onto `main` as a **deterministic A/B measuring instrument** — a "ruler"
that takes two Claude Code session transcripts (one produced with Anatomia, one
vanilla) and emits a tamper-evident, reproducible comparison: tokens, tool calls,
cost, and the reliability metrics that carry the product's "verified over trusted"
claim.

findings.summary: Dead export `totalTokens` — defined with full JSDoc, never called anywhere (cost uses derived.tokens; the edit-token walk inlines its own input+output sum)

aggregate treats 'lower is better' for every numeric metric, including distinctFilesRead/inputTokens/turns — an arm that reads fewer files (even by missing relevant ones) scores a 'win'. Spec-endorsed simplification, but a semantic foot-gun for the next reader.

AC7 guard (A036/A037) is a textual substring assertion on scorer.ts source — a future read of a best-effort field via destructuring-rename or computed key would slip past. Legitimate enforcement test, but textual not semantic.

The three to-first-correct-edit metrics can disagree on null-ness: wallClockMsToFirstCorrectEdit is null when the editing line lacks a parseable timestamp, even while turnsToResolution/tokensToFirstCorrectEdit are populated. Latent inconsistency; not triggered by the committed fixtures.

Determinism test (A034) only asserts same-input idempotence, not stability across input permutations. Implementation genuinely sorts by (task,metric,arm) so cross-permutation determinism holds, but the test would pass even if the explicit sort were removed.

Long-standing benign lint warning (NOT a failure): `pnpm run lint` passes exit 0 but emits one 'Unused eslint-disable directive (no-control-regex)' warning in src/utils/git-operations.ts:198. Root cause: commit 83b2446d [security-hardening] added the directive for a rule the cli eslint.config.js never enables (no @eslint/js recommended), so it was redundant from commit; ESLint v9+ reports redundant directives. Untouched by this build; flagged across ~12 prior cycles. Fix is a one-line deletion of the redundant directive (zero behavior change).

build_concerns.summary: A038 (anatrace-core unmodified) is a git/process gate, not a unit test — node_modules is untracked so no test can assert a git diff. Verified mechanically: `git diff --name-only -- '*anatrace-core*'` is empty and the scorer only imports/reads the package.

Spec mockup ci95.high for [1,2,3] is 4.484438 (uses a rounded t-critical); the implementation uses the precise textbook t(0.975, df=2)=4.302653, yielding ci95.high=4.48414. Contract A029 only asserts >3.2, which holds. The small numeric difference is correct, not a defect.

The scorer reads the transcript file three times (parseTranscript for localization lines, deriveTranscript for counts, and a third readFileSync for parseSession/analyze context bytes). The spec endorses the double parse for surface clarity; the third read is the added context-facts surface. Transcripts are tiny, so this is intentional, not a perf concern — flagged for visibility.

The pre-commit lint hook reports 1 warning (0 errors) over the staged set; the warning is not in any benchmark file (scoped eslint on the five new files is clean). Pre-existing, not introduced by this build.

entries.feature: Web proof page — Provenance & Session Attestation

assertions.says: The provenance view lists one row for every pipeline session
Each session's cache figure combines cache-create and cache-read tokens
The total cost is the sum of the priced sessions
The totals row reports how many sessions the run had
Code churn adds up the lines changed across all touched files
The completeness check reports whether every expected session was captured
Session attestation counts how many claims were unverifiable per agent
Session attestation shows how many of an agent's claims were actually checked
Session attestation records which engine version judged the run
When every session ran on one model, that model is shown once
A proof with no provenance data adds no Provenance entry to the page outline
A proof with no provenance data adds no Provenance text to the copyable page
A proof with no attestation data adds no Attestation entry to the page outline
A provenance-only proof still shows its Provenance section
A provenance-only proof does not show a Session Attestation section
Provenance data holds no pass or fail status — it cannot read as gating
Unverifiable claims are tallied separately from violations, not as failures
The coverage ratio is available for prominent display
A genuine violation is surfaced in the violated count
An unexercised veto reports as not applied
The veto explains itself with the reason recorded in the proof
An applied veto reports as applied
A session's cost comes from the shared price table, not a stored figure
An unpriced model shows no cost rather than a misleading zero
The totals row reports how many sessions could not be priced
The price-table version shown is the one the cost was computed against
When provenance exists, it appears in the copyable page text
When attestation is absent, no attestation text is added to the copyable page

entries.scope_summary: The web proof page (`website/app/docs/proof/[slug]/page.tsx`) is the product's signature public artifact — the data nobody else can produce. It renders Hero → Timeline → Assertion Ledger → Findings → Integrity Seal, but renders **none** of the process/behavioral data the 1.3.0 proof schema started producing: Provenance, Session Attestation, or the verdict-veto state. The CLI (`ana proof <slug>`) renders all of it, and the shipped prose (`website/content/docs/guides/reading-a-proof.mdx`) describes all of it — including the literal claim that "the proof page on the docs site shows the same data with more detail." That sentence is currently an over-claim. The web page must catch up to the prose and the CLI.

findings.summary: Markdown helpers asserted only by length, not content — format regressions (n/a total, churn line, separators) would pass unnoticed
The all-unpriced TOTAL='n/a' branch is never exercised — unpricedProcess() keeps session 0 priced, so totals.costUsd is never 0 while unpriced>0
Honest-unpriced path (n/a cost cell + n/a total) is dormant in the entire 210-entry corpus — 0 unpriced sessions, so the feature is correct and unit-tested at session level but unexercised end-to-end

JSX components (ProvenanceTable, SessionAttestation) have no automated tests for palette/positioning (AC4/AC5/AC6 visual dimension) — env limitation acknowledged in spec; verified by build success + code read only
JSDoc comment contains an ' HTML entity inside a plain JS block comment (ledger's) — unnecessary escaping, renders literally in source
Redundant guard: model field uses `allSameModel && rawSessions.length > 0` but allSameModel already requires length > 0 — dead sub-condition

build_concerns.summary: Extractor's dynamic import of packages/cli/src/data/pricing.ts is untyped (any); a rename of computeCost/PRICES in anatrace-core would not be caught at website compile time.

The unpriced-cost path (costUsd:null, totals.unpriced, 'n/a' render) is exercised only by unit tests — all 18 real provenance cohorts are fully priced, so the 'n/a' rendering is unverified against a real built page.

The verdict-veto applied:true 'serious treatment' JSX is unverified against real data — all 4 real veto records are applied:false. The data mapping is unit-tested (A022) but the alarm-styled render is craft-only.

Palette/positioning for AC4/AC5/AC6 (no verdict color on provenance; neutral unverifiable; restrained satisfied; violated-only alarm) is craft-reviewed, not unit-tested — the node-env Vitest does not collect component JSX. Needs a human read of the built page across the 5 cohorts.
